step_by_step_thinking:
Renal agenesis, or the absence of one or both kidneys, can lead to oligohydramnios because reduced fetal urine production results in decreased amniotic fluid volume. This condition often accompanies other congenital anomalies due to its association with malformations that affect multiple organ systems.

My answer is D: Oligohydramnios.
The renal system plays a crucial role in producing amniotic fluid through urinary output; therefore, when this function is absent as in renal agenesis, there will be significantly less amniotic fluid available for the developing fetus. Hence,
raw_response:
Renal agenesis, or the absence of one or both kidneys, can lead to oligohydramnios because reduced fetal urine production results in decreased amniotic fluid volume. This condition often accompanies other congenital anomalies due to its association with malformations that affect multiple organ systems.

My answer is D: Oligohydramnios.
The renal system plays a crucial role in producing amniotic fluid through urinary output; therefore, when this function is absent as in renal agenesis, there will be significantly less amniotic fluid available for the developing fetus. Hence, option D accurately reflects what would typically occur in such cases.
